item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whelp, that pegged my WTF meter this morning!

I love an article that takes off orthogonally to every direction I could possibly imagine it going. I just love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first read this I assumed it was a Series-II article I never read before, so I was really surprised when I checked the history. It blends in great with the Series-II list and perfectly replicates the feel of early scips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wait a second
waaaaait a mothergoddamn second
did i just
did you

item.title: Re: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded: Anyone trying to command it found themselves talking in a…very stilted… way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
occasional visual blackouts when viewing nonanomalous canopies, tents, and gazebos.

Wonder what this tent knows that we don't…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Gazebo is a creature from the Far Realm we can’t hope to comprehend. Mimics are but its foul spawn, pale and malformed imitations of their otherworldly mother’s true form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eyes, in keeping with the running theme. If the vector is sight, it almost makes sense that the eyes would be involved somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's a strange almost plausible creature

My disagreement with you begins here :p

It's a magic cretaure, and it reproduces by magic. Makes sense to me. Why it's eyes… I dunno. I guess testicles/ovaries would have worked just as well? But then there would be casualties…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I'm missing something but the article feel very disjointed, the thing eats things then peoples eyes plop out and make more of it? it's a strange almost plausible creature and a random "magic" effect mashed together, nothing about the theme here seem to justify it. It kinde feels like a random scary creature that does a random scary thing. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Screw you for making the Eric and the Dread Gazebo story an SCP

2) +1

3) You've finally inspired me to try to stat up a Gazebo monster. Question is, is it going to be some form of mimic, or another aberration?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You see a well-groomed garden. In the middle, on a small hill, is a gazebo."

But is there a fire mage in the party?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You see a well-groomed garden. In the middle, on a small hill, is a gazebo."

That's where my mind went, and I was pleased at how this turned out. Anomalous organisms that mimic unremarkable or innocuous objects can be quite unsettling if done right, and this was.

I definitely like that the eyeballs grow back. Eye Scream is a trope that really squicks me out.

item.title: Re: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded:
A foundation vessel, that just happened to have that number and had to be decommissioned because of certain apparitions appearing?

Also, to not derail the comments, I like the article, because it reminds me of the first series in a good way. It has behaviour of a biological being in a nonliving object, which apparently has to make up for that in a VERY strange way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely simplistic anomaly with interesting properties, made particularly interesting due to its reproductive ability. It's refreshing to see such articles every once in a while. My vote's obvious: +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved it up until the eyelashes turning into aluminum thing, and then the eyeballs popped out and got replaced and I loved it again.

What in the hell. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me stutter.

Because I had no idea what to say about this

+1

item.title: Re: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded:
It's on you to write SCP-1701-D "A decommissioned ship".

:P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck man, this is really awesome, for a really simple reason.

It's not just a "predatory canopy", it's one with a respiratory, and reproductive cycle.

Great attention to detail, and really great, short, effective horror. +1.

item.title: Re: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded:
I agree that it shouldn't, but it is. Star Trek at some point moved into the part of my brain that is occupied by religion in normal people.

I am trying to get over it and probably will upvote eventually.

item.title: Re: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded: Ah well. That shouldn't affect your vote? Though I sympathize; it is an eternal tragedy that say, SCP-1337 is about a ghost and not hackers.

item.title: NCC-1701...
item.content:encoded:
They both have the USS Enterprise, this article does not.

Or they would if autocorrect wasn't always changing things to make me look like an idiot.

item.title: Re: Maybe I can roll up a fire-mage
item.content:encoded: What would that reason be, pray tell? Also you're gonna have to explain the significance of those slots.

item.title: Maybe I can roll up a fire-mage
item.content:encoded:
I find myself unable to upvote this for a really awful, petty, reason.

If only this could swap places with either SCP-2063 or SCP-3135 :(

Edit: Goddammit autocorrect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly I upvoted because first, it takes your eyeballs, and you get them back. No caveats, changes, problems- it reproduces in your eyeballs and then makes sure you get them back.

Good guy anomalous tent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry you feel that way.

most modern articles

One of these words is really important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

I felt this article is just an idea without a story like most modern articles are told to be like. While this is a neat idea, I would dig seeing this expanded on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You didn't.

You did.

I am fuming.

item.title: Paladins Beware
item.content:encoded:
Stalled while drafting 4k. One windy day and one rogue EZ UP planted a seed in my mind and eventually I watered it.

Thanks to 9Volt and Captain Kirby for crit, and special thanks to CadaverCommander for his timeless wisdom and for making me push this further than I had it.

Pic is here, public domain: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:High_peak_frame_tent.JPG
